codeReview.summary: The feature adds a focused Playwright regression gate for Recover NSEC and directly verifies the two contract-critical UI behaviors called out in scope: zero console errors/Paper copy on recover screens and the masked↔revealed toggle behavior on success. The implementation is clean and test-only, and no blocking defects were found in this feature’s committed changes.

issues.description: The new gate validates seeded `/demo` states (`/demo/recover-collect-shares` and `/demo/recover-success`) but does not drive the live recover interaction path (`Validate Sources` -> `Recover NSEC`). The only full-browser recover journey remains `test.fixme` in `src/e2e/outside-runtime-flows.spec.ts:126`, so full-flow regressions can still slip past this specific spec.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance says no `.skip` additions, but this feature adds project-conditional `test.skip(...)` guards for desktop-only execution. This suggests the convention needs an explicit exception (or alternate pattern) for project-scoped Playwright specs.
The assigned skill requires RED-first evidence (new tests should fail before implementation), but this run recorded only passing post-addition executions and still marked `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md:33-37` requires failing-test-first evidence; handoff `2026-04-23T00-50-47-508Z__m7-recover-regression__4283e5f8-629e-481d-9569-03de3a7e4e2f.json` `verification.commandsRun` contains only successful runs after file creation, while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is `true`.

summary: Review result: PASS. Commit 6f0d65abd2c94b3245cd158be0a47f393739acc9 delivers a focused Recover NSEC regression gate for console cleanliness, Paper copy presence, and masked/revealed toggle behavior. One non-blocking coverage gap and two shared-state observations (skip-rule ambiguity and RED-first skill adherence drift) were recorded.
